Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","numLines":300,"startLine":1,"totalLines":300}}}

Typical Router Response\n26\t- **Current Manual**: 0.0004 ms (2.8M ops/sec)\n27\t- **Regex**: 0.0029 ms (350K ops/sec) - **7x slower**\n28\t- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.0050 ms (200K ops/sec) - **12x slower**\n29\t\n30\t#### Medium Payload (~500 bytes) - Typical Synthesize Response\n31\t- **Current Manual**: 0.0004 ms (2.5M ops/sec)\n32\t- **Regex**: 0.0064 ms (156K ops/sec) - **16x slower**\n33\t- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.0109 ms (91K ops/sec) - **27x slower**\n34\t\n35\t#### Large Payload (~13.1 KB) - Complex Multi-source Fetch Result\n36\t- **Current Manual**: 0.0009 ms (1.1M ops/sec)\n37\t- **Regex**: 0.1611 ms (6.2K ops/sec) - **179x slower** ⚠️\n38\t- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.2394 ms (4.2K ops/sec) - **266x slower** ⚠️\n39\t\n40\t**Critical insight:** Regex performance degrades catastrophically on larger payloads due to backtracking in the `[\\s\\S]+?` pattern.\n41\t\n42\t## Hot Path Locations\n43\t\n44\tJSON parsing is executed on **EVERY dispatch** through these hot paths:\n45\t\n46\t1. **src/intent/router.py** (lines 251-257)\n47\t   ```python\n48\t   raw = response.strip()\n49\t   if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n50\t       raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n51\t       raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n52\t   intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n53\t   ```\n54\t   - Runs on every utterance classification\n55\t   - ~1 classification per dispatch\n56\t\n57\t2. **src/synthesize/strand.py** (lines 143-150)\n58\t   ```python\n59\t   raw = response.strip()\n60\t   if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n61\t       raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n62\t       raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n63\t   result_data = json.loads(raw)\n64\t   ```\n65\t   - Runs on every intent synthesis\n66\t   - ~1 synthesis per intent thread\n67\t\n68\t3. **src/fetch/orchestrator.py** (lines with `_json.loads(stdout.decode())`)\n69\t   - Parses br CLI output from bead-forge\n70\t   - Runs on every fetch operation that queries br\n71\t\n72\t## Key Findings\n73\t\n74\t### ✅ Current Implementation is Optimal\n75\tThe manual string-splitting approach used in `router.py` and `synthesize.py` is **already the fastest**:\n76\t- **7x faster** than regex on small payloads\n77\t- **179x faster** than regex on large payloads\n78\t- No need for optimization or refactoring\n79\t\n80\t### ⚠️ Regex is Performance-Killing\n81\tThe precompiled regex pattern in `src/llm/response_parser.py` (`_FENCE_PATTERN`) causes severe performance degradation on larger payloads due to backtracking in `[\\s\\S]+?`:\n82\t- Small payloads: Acceptable (7x slower)\n83\t- Large payloads: Catastrophic (179x slower)\n84\t\n85\t**Recommendation:** Do NOT replace current manual implementation with regex-based alternatives.\n86\t\n87\t### 📊 Centralized Parser is Slower but Safer\n88\t`parse_llm_response()` in `src/llm/response_parser.py` is **170x slower** than the manual implementation but provides:\n89\t- Rich error messages with snippets\n90\t- Exception type (`ParseLLMError`)\n91\t- Fence stripping + JSON parsing in one call\n92\t- Consistent error handling across codebase\n93\t\n94\t**Trade-off decision:** Use manual implementation in hot paths (router, synthesize); use centralized parser where robust error handling is more important than raw speed.\n95\t\n96\t### 🔍 Other JSON Parsing Paths\n97\tThe codebase has ~25 other `json.loads()` call sites outside the LLM hot paths:\n98\t- Session store (context serialization)\n99\t- Topic model (project_slugs parsing)\n100\t- Fetch orchestrator (br CLI output)\n101\t- Feedback/background analysis\n102\t- Memory store\n103\t- Watcher daemon\n104\t\n105\tThese are **low-frequency** operations and do not represent performance bottlenecks.\n106\t\n107\t## Acceptance Criteria Status\n108\t\n109\t- ✅ **Performance measurements for key JSON parsing operations**\n110\t  - Typical Router Response\n- **Current Manual**: 0.0004 ms (2.8M ops/sec)\n- **Regex**: 0.0029 ms (350K ops/sec) - **7x slower**\n- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.0050 ms (200K ops/sec) - **12x slower**\n\n#### Medium Payload (~500 bytes) - Typical Synthesize Response\n- **Current Manual**: 0.0004 ms (2.5M ops/sec)\n- **Regex**: 0.0064 ms (156K ops/sec) - **16x slower**\n- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.0109 ms (91K ops/sec) - **27x slower**\n\n#### Large Payload (~13.1 KB) - Complex Multi-source Fetch Result\n- **Current Manual**: 0.0009 ms (1.1M ops/sec)\n- **Regex**: 0.1611 ms (6.2K ops/sec) - **179x slower** ⚠️\n- **Full parse_llm_response**: 0.2394 ms (4.2K ops/sec) - **266x slower** ⚠️\n\n**Critical insight:** Regex performance degrades catastrophically on larger payloads due to backtracking in the `[\\s\\S]+?` pattern.\n\n## Hot Path Locations\n\nJSON parsing is executed on **EVERY dispatch** through these hot paths:\n\n1. **src/intent/router.py** (lines 251-257)\n   ```python\n   raw = response.strip()\n   if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n       raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n       raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n   intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n   ```\n   - Runs on every utterance classification\n   - ~1 classification per dispatch\n\n2. **src/synthesize/strand.py** (lines 143-150)\n   ```python\n   raw = response.strip()\n   if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n       raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n       raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n   result_data = json.loads(raw)\n   ```\n   - Runs on every intent synthesis\n   - ~1 synthesis per intent thread\n\n3. **src/fetch/orchestrator.py** (lines with `_json.loads(stdout.decode())`)\n   - Parses br CLI output from bead-forge\n   - Runs on every fetch operation that queries br\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### ✅ Current Implementation is Optimal\nThe manual string-splitting approach used in `router.py` and `synthesize.py` is **already the fastest**:\n- **7x faster** than regex on small payloads\n- **179x faster** than regex on large payloads\n- No need for optimization or refactoring\n\n### ⚠️ Regex is Performance-Killing\nThe precompiled regex pattern in `src/llm/response_parser.py` (`_FENCE_PATTERN`) causes severe performance degradation on larger payloads due to backtracking in `[\\s\\S]+?`:\n- Small payloads: Acceptable (7x slower)\n- Large payloads: Catastrophic (179x slower)\n\n**Recommendation:** Do NOT replace current manual implementation with regex-based alternatives.\n\n### 📊 Centralized Parser is Slower but Safer\n`parse_llm_response()` in `src/llm/response_parser.py` is **170x slower** than the manual implementation but provides:\n- Rich error messages with snippets\n- Exception type (`ParseLLMError`)\n- Fence stripping + JSON parsing in one call\n- Consistent error handling across codebase\n\n**Trade-off decision:** Use manual implementation in hot paths (router, synthesize); use centralized parser where robust error handling is more important than raw speed.\n\n### 🔍 Other JSON Parsing Paths\nThe codebase has ~25 other `json.loads()` call sites outside the LLM hot paths:\n- Session store (context serialization)\n- Topic model (project_slugs parsing)\n- Fetch orchestrator (br CLI output)\n- Feedback/background analysis\n- Memory store\n- Watcher daemon\n\nThese are **low-frequency** operations and do not represent performance bottlenecks.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Status\n\n- ✅ **Performance measurements for key JSON parsing operations**\n  - Benchmarked 5 different approaches across 3 payload sizes and 3 fence formats\n  - 27 total benchmark configurations, 1000 iterations each\n\n- ✅ **Identification of slow operations (if any)**\n  - Identified regex-based fence stripping as slow (93-266x slower than baseline)\n  - Identified `parse_llm_response()` as slower than manual but acceptable for non-hot-path usage\n\n- ✅ **Baseline metrics documented for comparison**\n  - All metrics documented in this file\n  - Benchmark script committed for re-runnable comparisons\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions\n1. **No changes needed** to current implementation in `router.py` and `synthesize.py`\n2. **Keep benchmark script** for future regression testing\n3. **Document hot paths** for awareness during refactoring\n\n### Long-term Considerations\n1. **Avoid regex** for fence stripping in hot paths\n2. **Use centralized parser** for non-critical paths where error handling is valuable\n3. **Re-run benchmark** after any changes to parsing logic\n\n## Benchmark Output\n\nFull benchmark output saved for reference:\n```\nPAYLOAD: SMALL (~200 bytes)\n----------------------------------------\nCurrent Manual:     0.0004 ms avg\nManual with Regex:  0.0029 ms avg (7x slower)\nFull parse_llm:    0.0050 ms avg (12x slower)\n\nPAYLOAD: MEDIUM (~500 bytes)\n----------------------------------------\nCurrent Manual:     0.0004 ms avg\nManual with Regex:  0.0064 ms avg (16x slower)\nFull parse_llm:    0.0109 ms avg (27x slower)\n\nPAYLOAD: LARGE (~13.1 KB)\n----------------------------------------\nCurrent Manual:     0.0009 ms avg\nManual with Regex:  0.1611 ms avg (179x slower)\nFull parse_llm:    0.2394 ms avg (266x slower)\n```\n\n## Files Created\n\n- `bench_json_parsing.py` -
